text: Abstract Skeletal editing, as defined as the synthesis of core structures with functional sites missing and that require single or a few transactions for their construction, allows for fast diversification of the corresponding molecules. While achieved on common arenes using typically carbene or nitrene-type insertion and rearrangement reactions, skeletal editing in heteroarenes is also efficient and late stage for core modification. The skeletal editing of the most relevant heteroarene core, that of pyridines, is unknown to date and was therefore investigated. The work discloses the direct skeletal editing of pyridines with an atom-pair swap from CN to CC, leading to the formation of various substituted benzenes and naphthalenes in a highly modular fashion. The core transformation of parent pyridines was achieved in a single operational step by sequential steps of dearomatization, cycloaddition and rearomatizing retrocycloaddition reaction, affording desired products at various sites on the rings, determined by precursors in the cycloaddition reaction. The potential of this work for late stage skeletal diversification of drug molecules is highlighted with a few examples.
